medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Patient with a history of non-small cell lung cancer with metastasis to lymph nodes, status post VATS/right lower lobectomy, and previous chemotherapy, presents with progressively worsening dyspnea over the past 3 days with severe episodes. Patient notes episodes associated with substernal chest discomfort and increased need for home oxygen.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Non-small cell lung cancer, prostate cancer, small bowel desmoid tumor, left adrenal cortical adenoma, hypercholesterolemia, migraine headache, left inguinal hernia repair, appendectomy.
medical_history.family_history: Maternal grandfather possibly died of colon cancer. Mother had heart problems and congestive heart failure. Father had multiple sclerosis. Brother has no medical problems.

physical_examination.general_examination: Temp 97.7, BP 130/72, HR 83, RR 22, O2 saturation 95% on 2L NC. Patient in no acute distress, mildly tachypneic.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Cardiovascular: Regular rate and normal rhythm. Respiratory: Bronchial breath sounds on the right, intermittent wheezing on the left.
